description: Sales qualification playbook for the Lead-Ops AI agent. The agent should reference this playbook when scoring leads against MEDDIC criteria.

pillars.name: Metrics
pillars.description: The quantifiable measures of the economic impact or business value the customer expects to achieve. Metrics prove that solving the problem is worth the investment.
qualifying_questions: What KPIs will this solution directly impact?
What is the cost of the current problem in dollars per quarter?
What ROI do you expect within the first 12 months?
How are you measuring success today?
What would a 10% improvement in [metric] mean for your business?
signals_to_look_for: Specific dollar amounts mentioned in emails (e.g., '$2.3M in lost revenue')
ROI calculations or business case documents shared
References to KPI dashboards or performance reports
Quantified time savings (e.g., '40 hours/week saved')
Conversion rate or pipeline velocity numbers
red_flags: Prospect cannot quantify the business impact
No metrics discussed after 2+ meetings
Vague statements like 'we want to improve efficiency'

score_rubric.0.0: No metrics discussed. Prospect has not quantified the problem.
score_rubric.0.25: Prospect acknowledges a problem exists but cannot quantify it.
score_rubric.0.5: Vague metrics mentioned (e.g., 'we want to improve'). No specific numbers.
score_rubric.0.75: Specific metrics identified but not yet validated by the economic buyer.
score_rubric.1.0: Concrete, validated metrics with stakeholder agreement on expected ROI.

pillars.name: Economic Buyer
pillars.description: The individual with the authority and budget to approve the purchase. This is the person who can say 'yes' when everyone else says 'no'.
qualifying_questions: Who has final sign-off authority on this budget?
What is the approval process for a purchase of this size?
Have you made a purchase of similar magnitude before? Who approved it?
Is there a committee or board that needs to approve this?
What is your fiscal year timeline for budget allocation?
signals_to_look_for: C-suite or VP-level contact mentioned in email threads
References to 'getting approval from [executive name]'
Budget discussions or procurement process details
Meeting invitations that include senior leadership
Forwarded emails from executives showing interest
red_flags: No executive sponsor identified after discovery call
Contact says 'I need to check with my boss' repeatedly
Unable to identify who controls the budget
Prospect is a junior individual contributor with no purchasing authority

score_rubric.0.0: Economic buyer not identified. No budget authority contact.
score_rubric.0.25: Prospect mentions a boss or executive but no direct contact established.
score_rubric.0.5: Economic buyer identified by name but no direct engagement yet.
score_rubric.0.75: Economic buyer engaged in at least one meeting or email thread.
score_rubric.1.0: Economic buyer actively involved, budget confirmed, and timeline agreed.

pillars.name: Decision Criteria
pillars.description: The formal and informal criteria the customer will use to evaluate and compare solutions. Understanding these lets you position your solution against competitors.
qualifying_questions: What are your must-have requirements for this solution?
How will you evaluate different vendors?
What technical requirements must be met?
Are there compliance or security requirements we should know about?
What has caused previous evaluations to fail?
signals_to_look_for: RFP or RFI documents shared
Technical requirements lists in emails
References to competitor features or pricing
Security questionnaires or compliance checklists
Integration requirements with existing systems
red_flags: Prospect has no formal evaluation criteria
Criteria seem designed to favor a competitor
Constantly changing requirements mid-evaluation

score_rubric.0.0: No decision criteria identified. Prospect has not thought about evaluation.
score_rubric.0.25: Prospect has vague preferences but no formal criteria.
score_rubric.0.5: Some criteria identified but not comprehensive or documented.
score_rubric.0.75: Formal criteria documented; our solution aligns with most requirements.
score_rubric.1.0: Complete criteria understood; we meet or exceed all requirements with evidence.

pillars.name: Decision Process
pillars.description: The specific steps, timeline, and stakeholders involved in the customer's internal purchasing process. Knowing the process lets you forecast accurately.
qualifying_questions: What are the steps from evaluation to signed contract?
Who else needs to be involved in this decision?
What is your target timeline for making a decision?
Are there any legal or procurement reviews required?
Have you gone through a similar buying process before? How long did it take?
signals_to_look_for: Detailed timeline shared (e.g., 'Board meeting in Q2')
Introduction to procurement or legal teams
Multi-stakeholder meeting invitations
References to 'next steps' with specific dates
Pilot or POC discussions with defined success criteria
red_flags: No clear timeline or next steps
Prospect unwilling to introduce other stakeholders
Repeated delays without explanation
'We're just exploring right now' after multiple meetings

score_rubric.0.25: Prospect mentions a general process but no specifics or timeline.
score_rubric.0.5: Key steps identified but not all stakeholders are known.
score_rubric.0.75: Full process mapped with timeline; key stakeholders identified.
score_rubric.1.0: Process documented with dates, all stakeholders engaged, next steps confirmed.

pillars.name: Identify Pain
pillars.description: The specific business problem or pain that creates urgency for the customer to act now rather than later. Without pain, there is no urgency to buy.
qualifying_questions: What is the biggest challenge you're facing right now?
What happens if you don't solve this problem in the next 6 months?
How is this problem affecting your team's productivity?
Have you tried to solve this before? What happened?
What triggered you to start looking for a solution now?
signals_to_look_for: Emotional language about frustrations (e.g., 'our team is burning out')
Specific anecdotes about process failures
Urgency indicators (e.g., 'we need this before Q3')
Executive pressure mentioned (e.g., 'the board is asking about this')
Competitive pressure (e.g., 'our competitors already have this')
red_flags: No clear pain articulated — appears to be 'nice to have'
Pain is theoretical, not experienced ('we might need this someday')
No urgency or timeline pressure
Prospect is 'just doing research'

score_rubric.0.0: No pain identified. Prospect seems satisfied with status quo.
score_rubric.0.25: Minor inconvenience acknowledged but no urgency.
score_rubric.0.5: Clear pain identified but not quantified. Some urgency.
score_rubric.0.75: Significant pain with urgency. Prospect has tried and failed to solve it.
score_rubric.1.0: Critical, quantified pain with executive-level urgency and a hard deadline.

pillars.name: Champion
pillars.description: An internal advocate within the customer organization who has influence, supports your solution, and helps navigate the internal buying process.
qualifying_questions: Who internally is most excited about this solution?
Has anyone presented our solution to leadership on your behalf?
Do you have the ability to influence the purchase decision?
Who would benefit most from this solution in your organization?
Can you introduce us to the decision-maker directly?
signals_to_look_for: Contact proactively shares internal information
Contact forwards emails to other stakeholders on your behalf
References to internal presentations about your solution
Contact coaches you on internal politics or objections
Contact follows up without being prompted
red_flags: No single advocate — all contacts are passive
Champion has no influence in the organization
Champion is a contractor or consultant with no authority
Contact is helpful but explicitly says 'I have no influence'

score_rubric.0.0: No champion identified. All contacts are passive.
score_rubric.0.25: Friendly contact but no evidence of internal advocacy.
score_rubric.0.5: Engaged contact who expresses support but has not taken action.
score_rubric.0.75: Active champion who has presented to leadership or made introductions.
score_rubric.1.0: Powerful champion with influence, actively selling internally, coaching our team.

interpretation.0.0_to_0.25: DISQUALIFY — Lead does not meet minimum qualification criteria. Remove from pipeline.
interpretation.0.25_to_0.50: NURTURE — Lead shows some potential but is not ready for sales engagement. Add to nurture sequence.
interpretation.0.50_to_0.75: QUALIFIED — Lead meets basic criteria. Assign to AE for further development.
interpretation.0.75_to_1.0: HIGHLY QUALIFIED — Strong opportunity. Prioritize and fast-track.
